uses: Pachygenium parvum, a plant species native to South Africa, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
